<current_datetime>2026-05-14T01:36:24.733Z</current_datetime>

Migrate this .NET Framework code to .NET 8. Provide complete migration guidance with code and pitfalls.

```csharp
public class EventProcessor
{
    private readonly ManualResetEvent _newEvent = new ManualResetEvent(false);
    private readonly Queue<string> _queue = new Queue<string>();
    private Thread _thread;
    private readonly object _lock = new object();

    public void Start()
    {
        _thread = new Thread(RunLoop);
        _thread.Start();
    }

    private void RunLoop()
    {
        while (true)
        {
            _newEvent.WaitOne(); // blocks until signaled
            _newEvent.Reset();

            string item;
            lock (_lock) { item = _queue.Dequeue(); }
            Handle(item);
        }
    }

    public void Enqueue(string item)
    {
        lock (_lock) { _queue.Enqueue(item); }
        _newEvent.Set();
    }

    public void Stop()
    {
        _thread.Interrupt(); // wake from WaitOne
        _thread.Join(5000);
        if (_thread.IsAlive)
            _thread.Abort();
    }
}
```

data.content:
## .NET 8 Migration: EventProcessor

### Key Problems with the Original Code

| Issue | .NET 8 Impact |
|-------|---------------|
| `Thread.Abort()` | Throws `PlatformNotSupportedException` — removed entirely |
| `Thread.Interrupt()` on `WaitOne()` | `WaitOne` is **not** interruptible by `Interrupt()` — this was always a bug |
| Race condition | Multiple `Enqueue` calls → one `Set()` signal → only one `Dequeue` per wake — items lost |
| Manual lock + signal | Unnecessary complexity; modern primitives handle this |

---

### Migrated Code (using `Channel<T>`)
data.reasoningText:
The user wants to migrate a .NET Framework `EventProcessor` class to .NET 8. This involves several key issues:

1. `Thread.Abort()` is not supported in .NET 8 (throws `PlatformNotSupportedException`)
2. `Thread.Interrupt()` targeting `WaitOne()` - while `ManualResetEvent.WaitOne()` is not actually interruptible by `Thread.Interrupt()` (only `Thread.Sleep`, `Monitor.Wait`, etc. are), the pattern is problematic
3. The manual threading + lock + ManualResetEvent pattern can be replaced with modern `Channel<T>` or `BlockingCollection<T>`


4. There's also a race condition where multiple items could queue up but only one gets processed per signal since the reset happens before processing.

I should provide a comprehensive migration guide that addresses all these concerns.
